title: Pyrus cucphuongensis
taxonomy: Pyrus cucphuongensis is classified as a species of pear tree. It belongs to the family Rosaceae, which includes many other fruit-bearing trees. The genus Pyrus includes various species of pear trees, and Pyrus cucphuongensis is one of the unique species found in Vietnam. Pyrus cucphuongensis was described as a new species in 2017 by scientists, adding to the diversity of pear tree species. intro: Pyrus cucphuongensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae, commonly known as the Cuc Phuong pear tree. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to protect against chronic diseases such as heart disease and cancer. The bioactive constituents of Pyrus cucphuongensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and polysaccharides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Pyrus cucphuongensis are teas and infusions, which are often used in traditional medicine to promote digestive health and reduce inflammation.
